citations.quote: The duty to make reasonable adjustments requires a higher education provider to take such steps as it is reasonable to have to take to avoid the disadvantage.

findings.text: Anfal Elbushra holds a student licence for Bedroom B, Flat 05, Castlelaw House from 13 March to 25 August 2026 under a tenancy with Edinburgh Napier University.
As of 20 April 2026, the university recorded rent arrears of £5,526.66 and issued a Notice to Quit with a payment deadline of 9 May 2026.
Anfal applied for the Accommodation Hardship Fund and received a payment date notification, indicating engagement with university support processes.
The draft complaint is structured for an internal university complaint or SPSO referral, but the exact forum and procedural stage require human confirmation.

proposed_artifacts.content:
# Complaint regarding accommodation arrears and support

**Complainer:** Anfal Elbushra  
**Student Number:** [redacted]  
**Accommodation:** Bedroom B, Flat 05, Castlelaw House  
**Date:** [insert date]

## 1. What this complaint is about

I am a nursing student at Edinburgh Napier University. I hold a student licence for the above accommodation from 13 March 2026 to 25 August 2026 (source: NAP-SRC-0010). The university says I owe £5,526.66 in rent arrears as at 20 April 2026 and has served a Notice to Quit (source: evidence map synthesis). I do not accept that the arrears position is fair or that the university has properly considered my circumstances.

## 2. Background

I applied for the Accommodation Hardship Fund and received a payment date notification (source: NAP-SRC-0022). I have also provided bank statements showing my financial position (source: NAP-SRC-0012). Despite this, the university has pressed for payment and issued a Notice to Quit.

## 3. My concerns

### 3.1 Failure to make reasonable adjustments

I have disclosed financial hardship and my commuting difficulties from Glasgow. The university has a duty under the Equality Act 2010 to make reasonable adjustments. I do not believe it has done so. The decision to pursue arrears and eviction without a proper assessment of my support needs may be a failure of that duty.

### 3.2 Inadequate consideration of hardship

The hardship fund process appears to have been considered separately from the arrears enforcement. I was not given a clear explanation of how my application affected the arrears demand or the Notice to Quit.

### 3.3 Procedural fairness

I was not given an opportunity to be heard before the Notice to Quit was issued. The meeting on 10 April 2026 (source: NAP-SRC-0062) did not resolve matters, and I was not told what evidence was relied on.

## 4. What I want

- A review of the arrears calculation and the decision to serve a Notice to Quit.
- A proper assessment of my hardship application and its impact on the arrears.
- A reasonable payment plan or alternative accommodation arrangement.
- An apology and confirmation that my circumstances will be considered in line with the Equality Act.

## 5. Documents I rely on

- Tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0010)
- Arrears notice and Notice to Quit (evidence map)
- Hardship fund correspondence (NAP-SRC-0022)
- Bank statements (NAP-SRC-0012, redacted)
- Meeting summary 10 April 2026 (NAP-SRC-0062)

**Note:** This is a candidate draft. The forum (internal complaint, SPSO, or court) must be confirmed. Legal advice should be sought before lodging. All personal data must be redacted before sharing.
proposed_artifacts.title: Candidate Complaint Draft

proposed_tasks.instructions: Confirm whether the university's internal complaints procedure has been exhausted. If not, identify the correct stage and tailor the draft accordingly. If SPSO is the intended forum, verify jurisdiction requirements under the Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002.
proposed_tasks.title: Verify complaint forum and exhaustion of internal procedures

redaction_flags.description: Bank statement details (NAP-SRC-0012) contain full transaction history; only relevant entries should be included in final pack.
Student number and personal contact details appear in multiple sources; must be redacted or replaced with placeholder in public-facing documents.
Hardship fund application (NAP-SRC-0008) includes sensitive financial declarations; limit disclosure to necessary excerpts.

risk_flags.description: Draft may overstate legal obligations of the university without citing specific contractual or statutory duties beyond the Equality Act.
Privacy risk: draft includes personal financial data and student details; redaction required before sharing outside the matter.
Forum mismatch risk: draft blends internal complaint, SPSO, and potential court language; human must select and tailor to one forum.

summary: Candidate complaint draft for Anfal Elbushra's Edinburgh Napier accommodation arrears. Draft is evidence-led, citing tenancy agreement, arrears notices, hardship fund application, and bank statements. Separates supported facts from uncertain claims. Flags privacy risks, missing authority for legal conclusions, and need for human review before lodging.

uncertainties.description: Exact dates of arrears accrual and payment history are unclear from extracted sources; full bank statement analysis needed.
Whether the university's internal complaints procedure has been exhausted is not confirmed; SPSO jurisdiction may not yet attach.
The legal basis for challenging the arrears or the Notice to Quit is not fully developed; the draft relies on factual hardship rather than a clear legal defence.
